name: barbell side split squat
instructions: Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward.
Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps.
Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, bending at the knees and hips.
As you lower, push your knees out to the sides and keep your weight on your heels.
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing slightly outward. Hold a barbell across your upper back, resting it on your traps. Engage your core and keep your chest up as you lower your body down into a squat position, bending at the knees and hips. As you lower, push your knees out to the sides and keep your weight on your heels. Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground, then push through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Axial loading under fatigue may worsen asymmetrical curves. Prefer split-stance or supported variations.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Loaded spinal flexion or heavy axial loading should be avoided. Use supported variations.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
